_publications._publicationTitle: Modulation of Ras signaling alters the toxicity of hydroquinone, a benzene metabolite and component of cigarette smoke.

_contextSnippet: Deletion of RAS1 results in increased tolerance to HQ treatment, whereas deletion of RAS2 has no effect
resourceName: BY4743 background diploid yeast deletion strains
details: ras1Δ deletion resulted in increased tolerance to hydroquinone treatment, while ras2Δ deletion had no effect
